services.heading: Everything under one roof.
services.description: Most foundries cast your part and hand it off. We cast it, machine it, coat it, inspect it, and deliver it — from a single facility with a single point of contact. No outsourced steps. No quality gaps between suppliers. Faster turnaround and consistent results from first article to millionth.

items.title: DFM Review & Prototyping
items.description: Send your CAD files. We analyse geometry, simulate fill, and recommend design optimisations. First samples in 3–4 weeks, with full DFM feedback within 48 hours.

items.title: Hot-Chamber Die Casting
items.description: 12 automated machines with robotic extraction running 24 hours. Parts from 1 g to 3 kg in Zamak 2, 3, and 5 with as-cast tolerances to ±0.05 mm.

items.title: CNC Post-Processing
items.description: In-house milling, turning, drilling, threading, and deburring. Parts go from casting to machining without leaving the building — preserving traceability and cutting lead time.
items.specs: Full CNC · Tumbling · Tight secondary tolerances

items.title: Surface Treatment & Coating
items.description: Chromium plating, powder coating, wet painting, e-coating, and laser engraving. Class A cosmetic finishes from a chrome-ready as-cast surface at 0.8 Ra μm.
items.specs: Chrome · Powder coat · Wet paint · Laser engrave

items.title: Quality & Inspection
items.description: CMM-verified dimensions, SPC-monitored process control, and full PPAP documentation. ISO 9001 certified since 1993 — over 30 years of audited quality systems.
items.specs: ISO 9001 · CMM · SPC · PPAP · Full traceability

items.title: Assembly & Supply Chain
items.description: Sub-assembly, insert pressing, and multi-component assembly. Kanban delivery, forecast planning, and consignment stock — we ship finished products on your schedule.

services.diffDescription: Competitors outsource post-processing, coating, or assembly to third parties. Every handoff introduces delay, variation, and finger-pointing when something goes wrong. Our model eliminates all of that.

differentiators.title: Zero Handoffs
differentiators.text: Your part never leaves our facility between casting and shipment. No transport damage, no lost traceability, no communication gaps between suppliers.

differentiators.title: Consistent Quality
differentiators.text: One quality system governs every step. SPC data from casting feeds directly into machining parameters. Coating specs are verified against the same CMM that checked the casting.

differentiators.title: Single Responsible Partner
differentiators.text: One purchase order, one point of contact, one company accountable for the finished component. When your production line depends on our parts, that simplicity matters.

services.ctaDescription: Send your CAD files or specifications. We’ll tell you exactly what’s achievable and give you a quote within 48 hours.

whyZincPage.heading: Why zinc die-casting?
whyZincPage.intro: Zinc die-casting is a high-speed, high-precision manufacturing process that produces net-shape metal components with tolerances to ±0.05 mm, chrome-ready surface finish, and cycle times under 45 seconds. It outperforms aluminum and plastic injection moulding in geometric complexity, dimensional accuracy, and cost-efficiency at volume — delivering stronger parts with longer tool life and less secondary processing. For engineers designing components that demand tight tolerances, thin walls, or Class A cosmetic surfaces, zinc is the material that eliminates compromises.

advantages.label: Achievable Tolerances
advantages.description: Tighter as-cast accuracy than aluminum or plastic. Many features need no secondary machining at all, reducing cost and lead time.

advantages.label: Die Life
advantages.description: Zinc dies last 5–10× longer than aluminum dies. Lower tooling cost per part and fewer production interruptions for tool replacement.

advantages.label: Cycle Time
advantages.description: The fastest metal die-casting process. Hot-chamber technology keeps zinc molten in the machine, eliminating the ladling step that slows aluminum casting.

advantages.label: Surface Finish
advantages.description: Chrome-ready straight from the die. No polishing or additional surface preparation needed for decorative finishes.

advantages.label: Minimum Wall Thickness
advantages.description: Thinner walls than any other metal die-casting process. Enables intricate geometries and lightweight designs impossible in aluminum.

advantages.label: Recyclable
advantages.description: Zinc is infinitely recyclable with no loss of material properties. Lower melting point means less energy consumed per casting cycle.

comparison.heading: Zinc vs. the alternatives
comparison.description: Side-by-side comparison of zinc die-casting against aluminum, magnesium, and plastic injection moulding. The data explains why zinc wins when precision, surface quality, and tool economics are the deciding factors.

alloys.heading: Zamak alloy guide
alloys.description: Zamak alloys are zinc-aluminium alloys optimised for die-casting. The right alloy depends on your part’s mechanical requirements, surface finish needs, and casting complexity. We cast all four standard grades and advise on selection during DFM review.

rows.name: Zamak 2
rows.use: High-strength structural components, die-cast bearings, heavy-duty hardware

rows.name: Zamak 3
rows.use: General purpose — the most widely cast zinc alloy. Excellent plating characteristics.

rows.name: Zamak 5
rows.use: Higher strength than Zamak 3 with good ductility. Automotive and industrial components.

rows.name: Zamak 7
rows.use: Superior casting fluidity for thin-walled and complex-geometry parts. Lower magnesium for ductility.

items.question: What is zinc die-casting?
items.answer: Zinc die-casting is a manufacturing process where molten zinc alloy is injected under high pressure into a steel die. The hot-chamber process keeps the alloy molten inside the machine, enabling cycle times as fast as 10 seconds. The result is a net-shape metal component with tight tolerances, excellent surface finish, and high repeatability — suitable for production volumes from hundreds to millions of parts.

items.question: Is zinc die-casting stronger than aluminium?
items.answer: Yes, in terms of tensile strength. Zamak alloys range from 283 to 397 MPa, while most die-cast aluminium alloys deliver 150–300 MPa. Zinc also achieves tighter as-cast tolerances (±0.05 mm vs. ±0.1 mm) and produces chrome-ready surfaces without secondary polishing. However, aluminium is lighter (2.7 g/cm³ vs. 6.6 g/cm³), so the choice depends on whether strength-to-weight ratio or absolute precision is the priority.

items.question: What is a Zamak alloy?
items.answer: Zamak is a family of zinc-aluminium alloys specifically developed for die-casting. The name comes from the German abbreviations of its constituents: Zink, Aluminium, Magnesium, and Kupfer (copper). The four standard grades — Zamak 2, 3, 5, and 7 — offer different balances of strength, ductility, and castability. Zamak 3 is the most widely used, while Zamak 2 provides the highest strength.

items.question: How much does zinc die-casting cost?
items.answer: Tooling cost depends on part complexity and number of cavities, typically ranging from €10,000 to €80,000. However, zinc dies last over 1 million shots — 5–10× longer than aluminium dies — dramatically reducing per-part tooling cost at volume. Piece prices depend on part weight, alloy, cycle time, and secondary operations. Send us your drawing for a specific quote — we respond within 48 hours.

items.question: What industries use zinc die-casting?
items.answer: Zinc die-cast components are used across automotive (structural and interior parts), furniture and design (visible fittings, handles), lighting (brackets, housings, heat sinks), building and HVAC (window fittings, motor frames), consumer electronics (precision housings), and industrial equipment (ergonomic handles, brackets). Any application requiring tight tolerances, complex geometry, or decorative surface finish is a candidate.

items.question: Can zinc die-cast parts be chrome plated?
items.answer: Yes, and this is one of zinc’s strongest advantages. Zinc die-castings achieve 0.8 Ra μm surface finish straight from the die — smooth enough for direct chrome plating without intermediate polishing. This makes zinc the preferred material for visible, decorative components in furniture, lighting, and consumer products where a flawless chrome finish is mandatory.

items.question: Is zinc die-casting environmentally friendly?
items.answer: Zinc is 100% recyclable with no degradation of material properties. Its lower melting point (385°C vs. 660°C for aluminium) means less energy per casting cycle. At Linimatic, virtually all casting scrap — runners, overflows, and reject parts — is remelted and reused directly in production. We also offer lead-free zinc alloys through our Zinkers product line.

items.question: What is the difference between zinc and Zamak?
items.answer: Zinc is the base element. Zamak is a specific family of zinc alloys engineered for die-casting, containing precisely controlled amounts of aluminium (3.5–4.3%), magnesium, and in some grades, copper. Pure zinc is too soft for most engineering applications — the alloying elements in Zamak provide the strength, hardness, and castability that make precision die-casting possible.

whyZincPage.ctaHeading: Still deciding? Talk to our engineers.
whyZincPage.ctaDescription: We’ll tell you honestly whether zinc is right for your part — or recommend an alternative if it isn’t. No sales pressure, just engineering expertise.

aboutPage.heading: Denmark’s largest dedicated zinc die-casting foundry.

story.p1: Linimatic was founded in 1967 in Helsinge, North Zealand, with a single purpose: to master zinc die-casting. Not as a sideline to aluminium or plastics, but as the sole focus of the entire operation. Nearly six decades later, that focus has made us Denmark’s largest dedicated zinc foundry — and the partner manufacturers turn to when the geometry is too complex, the tolerances too tight, or the surface requirements too demanding for other foundries to quote.
story.p2: We built the full value chain under one roof because the alternative — outsourcing machining to one supplier, coating to another, assembly to a third — creates gaps. Gaps in quality, gaps in accountability, gaps in delivery schedules. Our model eliminates all of that. One facility, one quality system, one team responsible from first sample to final shipment.
story.p3: "The best for the most complex" is not a marketing line. It’s a filter we apply to every investment decision, every hire, every machine purchase. We don’t compete on being the cheapest. We compete on being the partner you trust when the part absolutely has to be right.

aboutPage.facilityAlt: Linimatic production facility in Helsinge, Denmark

certifications.name: ISO 9001 — Quality Management
certifications.detail: Certified since 1993 — over 30 years of independently audited quality management. Covers the complete value chain from incoming material through casting, machining, surface treatment, assembly, and final inspection.

certifications.name: Environmental Compliance
certifications.detail: Compliant with EU environmental regulations including REACH and RoHS. Active waste reduction and energy management programmes. Zinc scrap is recycled directly in production with near-zero material waste.

certifications.name: Material Certifications
certifications.detail: Full material traceability on all Zamak alloys. Incoming material certificates verified against specification. Batch-level traceability from raw material through to finished component.

sustainability.p1: Zinc is infinitely recyclable. At Linimatic, virtually all casting scrap — runners, overflows, and reject parts — is remelted and fed back into production. Our closed-loop recycling means near-zero zinc waste leaves the facility. The low melting point of zinc (385°C vs. 660°C for aluminium) translates directly to lower energy consumption per casting cycle.
sustainability.p2: We invest in energy-efficient equipment, LED facility lighting, and heat recovery systems. Our lead-free Zinkers product line demonstrates that environmental responsibility and product performance are not trade-offs. For procurement teams with sustainability reporting requirements, we provide verified data on recycling rates, energy consumption, and waste metrics.
